project_type: Static HTML marketing site for insurance products (Manulife)
build_system: None - static files served directly. No build step needed.
test_framework: None - static HTML pages. Validation done by checking HTML syntax and opening in browser.
test_command: N/A - static site, validate HTML structure manually
verification_instructions: 1. Verify file exists at public/pages/mdsa.html. 2. Verify it loads /assets/style.css and /assets/main.js. 3. Verify the premium calculator JavaScript runs without errors (no syntax errors). 4. Verify the page follows the mccp.html pattern with sections, hero, premium tables, calculator, FAQ.
setup_instructions: No setup needed - the project is a collection of static HTML/CSS/JS files.
environment_constraints: INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network - no external dependencies can be downloaded. All resources must be self-contained or reference local paths. Google Fonts referenced in style.css won't load in sandbox but that's OK for production.
contribution_requirements: Follow existing page patterns. Pages use /assets/style.css as base, /assets/main.js for component loading (header/footer), and have page-specific inline styles in a <style> block.
key_patterns:
1. Each product page follows the pattern in public/pages/mccp.html: imports /assets/style.css, has inline page-specific CSS, uses
and
placeholders, includes sections with .container class, uses CSS variables from the design system (--jade, --bg-card, --txt-head, etc.), uses keyword highlight classes (kw, kw-cta, kw-gold), and loads /assets/main.js at the end. 2. Premium data is embedded as a JavaScript object/constant. 3. Calculator uses pure JSON lookup approach, NOT mathematical formulas. 4. Content should use question-based language to engage users. 5. Keywords highlighted with different colors.
relevant_files: public/pages/mccp.html (pattern to follow for page structure/styling)
public/assets/style.css (base design system with CSS variables)
Sample/Mdsa.html (COMPLETE data source - premium data, calculator logic, product details, riders, FAQ)
directory_structure: public/pages/ - product pages; public/assets/ - shared CSS/JS; public/components/ - header.html/footer.html; Sample/ - reference HTML files with full product data
